subject: History
chapter: Bricks, Beads and Bones: The Harappan Civilisation

questions.q: Describe the major features of the Harappan civilization's urban planning and architecture. How did these contribute to the rise of a complex urban society? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The Harappan civilization's urban planning and architecture were characterized by well-planned streets, public buildings, and residential areas. The cities were divided into different sections, such as the citadel and the lower town. The citadel was the seat of power and housed the rulers, while the lower town was the commercial and residential area. The cities were surrounded by high walls and had a sophisticated drainage system. The architecture was characterized by the use of sun-dried bricks, stone, and timber. The buildings were designed to withstand earthquakes and were often decorated with carvings and sculptures. The major features of the Harappan architecture include the Great Bath, the Granary, and the Assembly Hall. These features contributed to the rise of a complex urban society by providing a sense of community, economic stability, and social hierarchy.
marking_scheme: Description of urban planning and architecture (1 mark)
Contribution to the rise of a complex urban society (2 marks)
Use of materials and design features (1 mark)
Examples of significant buildings (1 mark)

questions.q: What were the major crops grown in the Harappan civilization? How did agriculture contribute to the growth of the civilization? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The major crops grown in the Harappan civilization were wheat, barley, and cotton. Agriculture played a crucial role in the growth of the civilization by providing food for the population and contributing to the economy. The Harappans developed a sophisticated irrigation system that allowed them to cultivate crops in areas with limited water supply. They also used advanced tools and techniques, such as the plough and the sickle, to increase crop yields. The surplus food produced by agriculture allowed the Harappans to support a large population, build complex cities, and develop a sophisticated trade network.
marking_scheme: Major crops grown in the Harappan civilization (1 mark)
Contribution of agriculture to the economy (1 mark)
Irrigation system and agricultural tools (1 mark)
Impact on population growth and urbanization (1 mark)
Role in trade and commerce (1 mark)

questions.q: Compare the Indus Valley script with the writing systems of ancient Mesopotamia and ancient Egypt. How did the Harappans use their writing system? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The Indus Valley script is a script used by the Harappans to write their language. It is a logographic script, meaning that it consists of symbols that represent words or concepts. The script is unique and has not been deciphered yet. The writing system of ancient Mesopotamia is cuneiform, which is a pictorial script that was used to write the Sumerian, Akkadian, and Babylonian languages. The writing system of ancient Egypt is hieroglyphics, which is a pictorial script that was used to write the ancient Egyptian language. The Harappans used their writing system to record economic transactions, administrative records, and other important information. They also used the script to label goods and commodities.
marking_scheme: Indus Valley script and its characteristics (1 mark)
Comparison with Mesopotamian and Egyptian writing systems (1 mark)
Use of the writing system by the Harappans (1 mark)
Types of information recorded using the script (1 mark)
Role of the script in the economy and administration (1 mark)

questions.q: A Harappan city has a population of 50,000 people. The average household size is 5 people. The city has 10,000 houses. Each house has a courtyard with a size of 10m x 10m. Calculate the total area of the courtyards. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Total area of courtyards = Number of houses x Area of courtyard per house = 10,000 x 100m² = 1,000,000m².
marking_scheme: Number of houses (1 mark)
Area of courtyard per house (1 mark)
Calculation of total area (2 marks)
Unit of measurement (1 mark)

questions.q: A Harappan farmer harvests 100 kg of wheat from a field. The average yield per hectare is 2000 kg. The field is 1 hectare in size. Calculate the yield per hectare. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Yield per hectare = Total yield / Number of hectares = 100 kg / 1 hectare = 2000 kg/hectare.
marking_scheme: Total yield (1 mark)
Number of hectares (1 mark)
Calculation of yield per hectare (2 marks)
Unit of measurement (1 mark)

questions.q: The average annual rainfall in a Harappan city is 500 mm. The city has a population of 50,000 people. The average water consumption per person per day is 50 liters. Calculate the total water consumption per year. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Total water consumption per year = Population x Water consumption per person per day x Number of days in a year = 50,000 x 50 liters/day x 365 days/year = 9,162,500 liters/year.
marking_scheme: Average annual rainfall (1 mark)
Population of the city (1 mark)
Water consumption per person per day (1 mark)
Number of days in a year (1 mark)
Calculation of total water consumption (1 mark)
